records.ety: [OE. drope, AS. dropa; akin to OS. dropo, D. drop, OHG. tropo, G. tropfen, Icel. dropi, Sw. droppe; and Fr. AS. drepan to drip, drop; akin to OS. driopan, D. druipen, OHG. triofan, G. triefen, Icel. drjūpa. Cf. Drip, Droop.]

defs: The quantity of fluid which falls in one small spherical mass; a liquid globule; a minim; hence, also, the smallest easily measured portion of a fluid; a small quantity; as, a drop of water.
quotes: With minute drops from off the eaves.
As dear to me as are the ruddy drops That visit my sad heart.

defs: That which resembles, or that which hangs like, a liquid drop; as a hanging diamond ornament, an earring, a glass pendant on a chandelier, a sugarplum (sometimes medicated), or a kind of shot or slug.
Same as Gutta.
Any small pendent ornament.
Whatever is arranged to drop, hang, or fall from an elevated position; also, a contrivance for lowering something
A door or platform opening downward; a trap door; that part of the gallows on which a culprit stands when he is to be hanged; hence, the gallows itself.
A machine for lowering heavy weights, as packages, coal wagons, etc., to a ship's deck.
A contrivance for temporarily lowering a gas jet.
A curtain which drops or falls in front of the stage of a theater, etc.
A drop press or drop hammer.
The distance of the axis of a shaft below the base of a hanger.
Any medicine the dose of which is measured by drops; as, lavender drops.
The depth of a square sail; -- generally applied to the courses only.
Act of dropping; sudden fall or descent.

records.ety: [OE. droppen, AS. dropan, v. i. See Drop, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dropped (?) or Dropt; p. pr. & vb. n. Dropping.]

defs: To pour or let fall in drops; to pour in small globules; to distill.
quotes: The recording angel, as he wrote it down, dropped a tear upon the word and blotted it out forever.

defs: To cause to fall in one portion, or by one motion, like a drop; to let fall; as, to drop a line in fishing; to drop a courtesy.
To let go; to dismiss; to set aside; to have done with; to discontinue; to forsake; to give up; to omit.
quotes: That astonishing ease with which fine ladies drop you and pick you up again.
The connection had been dropped many years.
Dropping the too rough H in Hell and Heaven.

defs: To bestow or communicate by a suggestion; to let fall in an indirect, cautious, or gentle manner; as, to drop hint, a word of counsel, etc.
To lower, as a curtain, or the muzzle of a gun, etc.
To send, as a letter; as, please drop me a line, a letter, word.
To give birth to; as, to drop a lamb.
To cover with drops; to variegate; to bedrop.
quotes: Show to the sun their waved coats dropped with gold.

defs: To fall in drops.
quotes: The kindly dew drops from the higher tree, And wets the little plants that lowly dwell.

defs: To fall, in general, literally or figuratively; as, ripe fruit drops from a tree; wise words drop from the lips.
quotes: Mutilations of which the meaning has dropped out of memory.
When the sound of dropping nuts is heard.

defs: To let drops fall; to discharge itself in drops.
quotes: The heavens . . . dropped at the presence of God.

defs: To fall dead, or to fall in death; as, dropping like flies.
quotes: Nothing, says Seneca, so soon reconciles us to the thoughts of our own death, as the prospect of one friend after another dropping round us.

defs: To come to an end; to cease; to pass out of mind; as, the affair dropped.
To come unexpectedly; -- with in or into; as, my old friend dropped in a moment.
quotes: Takes care to drop in when he thinks you are just seated.

defs: To fall or be depressed; to lower; as, the point of the spear dropped a little.
To fall short of a mark.
quotes: Often it drops or overshoots by the disproportion of distance.

defs: To be deep in extent; to descend perpendicularly; as, her main topsail drops seventeen yards.
